title: Talking like a local
setting: You run into Roberto on the street. He greets you in full casual Nica mode.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.en: Hey, dude! What's up with you?

options.feedback: Perfect register — 'maje' back at him plus the casual 'aquí nomás.' You sound local.

options.en: I'm fine, thank you. How are you?
options.feedback: Way too formal with a buddy! 'Usted' with Roberto sounds stiff and distant here.
'En la lucha' (in the struggle/grind) is a great casual answer. Very natural.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: Fijate que hoy hay juntada donde Beto. ¿Te apuntás?
steps.en: So there's a get-together at Beto's today. You in?

options.feedback: 'Dale pues' is the ideal casual yes, and you got the practical detail. Smooth.
Careful — to a Nica ear 'ahí veo' often signals a soft no. If you mean yes, say it!

options.es: Sí, me gustaría asistir al evento.
options.en: Yes, I'd like to attend the event.
options.feedback: Grammatically perfect but stiff — 'asistir al evento' for a house party sounds like a textbook.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: ¡Tuani! Va a estar deacachimba la cosa.
steps.en: Awesome! It's gonna be a great time.

options.en: Great! I'll be there for sure.
options.feedback: Enthusiastic and natural. 'Sin falta' (without fail) shows you're committed.

options.en: How nice that it'll be fun.
options.feedback: A little flat next to his energy. Match his vibe — get hyped!
Kind of a downer when he's hyping it up. Read the energy and roll with it.

steps.speaker: Roberto
steps.es: De show, maje. Nos vemos al rato.
steps.en: Cool, dude. See you in a bit.

options.en: See you there, dude. Take care.
options.feedback: Casual, warm, perfectly matched to him. You closed it like a true compa.

options.es: Hasta luego, que tenga buen día.
options.feedback: Polite but formal again — with Roberto, keep it loose: 'nos vemos, maje.'
Easy and natural — 'al rato pues' is exactly how friends sign off here.
